commit.message:
Fix cables: convert BezierCurves to meshes for RealityKit

RealityKit doesn't support USD BasisCurves primitives. Convert all 371
cable curves to mesh geometry in Blender before USDZ export so they
render as visible tube meshes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Z-fighting comprehensively and fix accumulator cloning axis

- Recess Plane.297 face vertices 5mm on both left and right sides
- Push 167 left-side and 147 right-side detail objects 3mm outward (8mm total clearance)
- Fix back-wall Z-fighting: recess 21 cube back-faces, push 21 circle objects
- Fix accumulator cloning: use position.z (not position.y) for row spacing
  Blender Y axis maps to RealityKit -Z after Z-up to Y-up conversion
- Fresh clean re-export of all 18 USDZ collections with recalculated normals

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fits page width without scrolling. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Replace Mermaid diagram with styled HTML/CSS architecture diagram Mermaid couldn't produce uniform-sized, centered nodes. HTML/CSS grid gives pixel-perfect control: - 3 centered rows: Zones (blue), Pillars (teal), Regulations (deep blue) - Uniform card sizes with flex layout - Color-coded by layer using brand tokens - Responsive at 600px breakpoint - Dark mode compatible via brand token overrides Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Replace placeholder logo with FSI shield + circuit motif - Shield shape: universal compliance/security symbol - Circuit/network nodes: represents AI agent governance - Gradient: Microsoft Blue #0078D4 to deep blue #005A9E - Matches site color palette - Applied to both logo.svg and favicon.svg Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Invert logo: white shield with blue circuit for header visibility Logo (nav bar): white shield pops against blue header bar, blue circuit nodes provide brand color. Same pattern as Azure Portal, M365 Admin. Favicon (browser tab): keeps blue gradient shield with white nodes since it renders on light tab backgrounds. Nodes enlarged and lines thickened for small-size legibility. in a single paragraph now have visual breathing room: - margin-top: 0.4em between fields - Labels colored with primary brand color for scannability Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Remove dead link to agent-365-architecture.md in framework index Content was consolidated into agent-identity-architecture.md. commit.message:
fix: match player_name with bridge suffix when persisting state

Runtime player names include ' @ bridge' suffix (e.g. 'ENEBY Portable @ LXC')
but config stores bare names ('ENEBY Portable'). Added _match_player_name()
helper to handle prefix matching in persist_device_enabled and
persist_device_released.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
Fix NRE in AddUniversalDynamicSymbol for invalid symbol address ranges (#2376)

When a ProcessSymbol event has an invalid address range (e.g.,
StartAddress=0x0, EndAddress=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF from zeroed
/proc/kallsyms on Linux without root), the ETLX conversion crashed
with a NullReferenceException.

Three fixes applied:
1. Skip symbols where EndAddress <= StartAddress (invalid range)
2. Change (int) to (long) cast for the size parameter to match
   ForAllUnresolvedCodeAddressesInRange's parameter type
3. Add null guard on loadedModule as defense-in-depth

Fixes #2373

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: read HA user display name from Ingress headers instead of showing 'HA User'

Supervisor sends X-Remote-User-Display-Name / X-Remote-User-Name headers
on every Ingress request (HA 2024.x+). Read these to show the actual
HA user's name in the header. Falls back to MA_USERNAME / 'HA User'
for older HA versions without session user data.

status cards with a simple enabled checkbox in Configuration → Devices.

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
fix: stop daemon subprocess on device disable to remove MA player

When disabling a device via POST /api/device/enabled, call
set_bt_management_enabled(False) on the active client. This stops the
daemon subprocess which disconnects from MA WebSocket, causing MA to
fire ClientRemovedEvent and unregister the player immediately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
feat: two-tier enabled/disabled — global device on/off with separate BT release

Redesign the enabled flag into a two-tier system:

1. Global enabled (config, requires restart): when enabled=false, no
   SendspinClient, BluetoothManager, port, or MA registration is created.
   Device metadata stored in state for UI display.

2. BT Release/Reclaim (hot toggle): existing functionality, only
   available when globally enabled. Releases BT adapter to host OS.

Changes:
- state.py: add disabled_devices storage (set/get with thread safety)
- sendspin_client.py: skip disabled devices entirely in main() loop
- routes/api_status.py: include disabled_devices in /api/status response
- routes/api_bt.py: add POST /api/device/enabled endpoint (restart_required)
- static/app.js: render disabled devices section, add Disable button
- templates/index.html: add #disabled-devices-section container
- static/style.css: dimmed/grayscale styling for disabled cards
- tests: 7 new tests covering state, API, and endpoint validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
fix: set audio_streaming=True on stream start (not only format_change)

audio_streaming was only set True in _handle_format_change, which
doesn't fire on re-anchor or track change when the codec/format is
unchanged. After stop→start, audio_streaming stayed False even though
audio was flowing — causing red equalizer indicator despite working
audio.

Now also set audio_streaming=True on stream 'start' event when
audio_format is already configured (re-anchor, track change).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
fix: correct PA sink routing after audio starts

PulseAudio may ignore PULSE_SINK env var and route sink-inputs to
the default sink instead (all python3.12 processes share the same
application.name in module-stream-restore). After audio_streaming
becomes True, move our PID's sink-inputs to the correct sink.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hub): add OIDC authentication scaffolding (#100)

  auth middleware, and auth routes (login, callback, logout, me)

  /auth/me endpoints outside the auth-protected API nest

Full Azure Entra ID integration (discovery, token validation, PKCE)
requires client registration and is out of scope for this scaffold.

commit.message:
docs: fix all shovel-readiness audit gaps (73→90+ target)

Fixes 15 implementation blockers, 9 ambiguities, 7 cross-doc
inconsistencies, and 8 missing-glue integration points.

ARCHITECTURE.md:
- B1: Replace broken JSON.stringify replacer with recursive canonicalize()
  function (full implementation provided)
- B2/B3: Add AgentRepository and RecordResult type definitions; repo holds
  identity for signing and optional firehose for event emission
- B4: Add Firehose container type (seq, log, subscriptions Map)
- A1: Specify filter as AND semantics
- C2: Fix phantom POSTED/DISCOVERED states in diagram → use actual status
  values (open, claimed, etc.) with explanatory note
- C3: Add overallScore to AggregatedReputation
- C4: Fix Llama 3.1 → Llama 3 typo
- C5: Add note that SCHEMAS.md is authoritative for full types
- C6: Fix taskBreakdown type to match IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN
- A3: Remove htmx/Preact → vanilla JS+CSS+SSE
- Base64url: specify Buffer.toString('base64url'), no package needed
- Mayor/Orchestrator terminology note and supervisor/labeler reserved note

IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:
- B1: Fix signing algorithm to use canonicalize()
- B2/B3: Update putRecord signature, add upsert semantics note
- B5: Add concrete requiredCapabilities table for all 8 tasks;
  add mock claim/completion data generation strategy with TASK_ARTIFACTS map
- B8: Add step-by-step rework/rejection flow (6 steps)
- B9: Add assessment score→string mapping table (5 ranges)
- B10/B11: Define Mayor type, createMayor() signature, AgentRegistryEntry,
  in-memory registry update logic (6 firehose event handlers)
- B12: Specify agent.state lifecycle (who creates/updates and when)
- B15: Add maxConcurrentTasks column to agent roster (6 agents + mayor)
- C1: Fix task 5/6 assignment swap (forge→profile cards, atlas→firehose UI)
- C7: Add DASHBOARD_PORT and DASHBOARD_SSE_EVENT_NAME to constants
- A2: Clarify multiple claims don't require state=open
- A4: Specify putRecord as upsert
- A6: Add SSE event format spec
- A7: Clarify createStamp computes overallScore internally
- G3: Add dependency-gated posting pseudocode
- G4: Add full createStamp parameter mapping (8 params)
- G6: Clarify getStampsForAgent scans firehose.log
- G8: Add checkAndPostUnblockedTasks logic
- Add npm scripts for package.json
- Trend for 0-1 stamps: always stable
- Node.js 20 LTS requirement

INTELLIGENCE.md:
- B13: Add explicit proficiency column to capability table
  (forge: react=intermediate, api=intermediate, db=beginner)
- Add proficiency levels for all 18 agent capabilities

feat(web): wire API layer to hub HTTP endpoints (#98)

Replace webFallback() stub returns with real fetch calls to the hub
service at /api/v1/*. All read operations (contexts, pods, resources,
events, logs, helm, metrics, CRDs, search, etc.) now hit the hub.
Write operations (scale, delete, apply, exec, port-forward, helm
rollback) are deferred to a future iteration.

Hub URL defaults to http://localhost:3001 and can be overridden via
window.__TELESCOPE_HUB_URL__.

commit.message:
feat: add telescope-hub axum HTTP server (#102)

Headless HTTP/WebSocket server exposing Telescope engine capabilities
to web clients via REST API, replacing Tauri IPC.

Implements 15 endpoints:
- GET /healthz — health check
- GET /api/v1/contexts — list kubeconfig contexts
- POST /api/v1/connect — connect to a context
- POST /api/v1/disconnect — disconnect
- GET /api/v1/connection-state — current connection state
- GET /api/v1/resources — list resources by GVK
- GET /api/v1/pods — list pods
- GET /api/v1/events — list events
- GET /api/v1/namespaces — list namespaces
- GET /api/v1/pods/{ns}/{name}/logs — pod logs
- GET /api/v1/cluster-info — cluster version/auth info
- GET /api/v1/search — search cached resources
- GET /api/v1/helm/releases — list Helm releases
- GET /api/v1/metrics/pods — pod metrics
- GET /api/v1/crds — list CRDs
- GET /ws — WebSocket endpoint (echo/placeholder)

Includes Dockerfile for containerized deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent false zombie restarts when audio is actually playing

Zombie detection now only triggers when audio has NEVER arrived in
the current subprocess session. Brief streaming gaps (re-anchor,
group sync, track changes) no longer cause false positives — PA
buffers keep playing during these gaps so restarting is wrong.

Reverted timeout back to 15s since it now only applies to genuinely
stuck subprocesses that never received audio data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: mute PA sinks directly on shutdown instead of pausing players

Replace subprocess pause command with direct PA sink muting in
_graceful_shutdown(). This ensures audio cuts cleanly regardless of
how the restart is triggered (systemd, Docker, HA auto-update, CLI)
instead of relying on the UI to pre-mute before restart.

commit.message:
docs: switch intelligence providers to GitHub Models (cloud) + Ollama (local)

Replace 3-provider setup (Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama) with a 2-provider
architecture for prototyping:
- GitHub Models: unified cloud gateway for Claude, GPT, Phi, and other
  commercial models available via GitHub's public catalog
- Local Ollama: self-hosted inference for open-source models (Llama 3 70B, CodeLlama)

Changes across all PRD docs:
- ARCHITECTURE.md: updated IntelligenceProvider/Model types (added endpoint,
  modelOrigin fields); added MVP Provider Strategy section; updated bootstrap flow
- E2E-SCENARIO.md: updated provider tree, model counts, all intelligence
  attribution strings, character sheets, and dashboard widget labels
- SCHEMAS.md: updated provider example to GitHub Models; added modelOrigin
  to IntelligenceModel interface and JSON example; updated providerDid DIDs
- IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md: updated bootstrap mock data strategy (2 providers, 6 models)
- README.md: updated Intelligence as Core Primitive section with 2-provider rationale

modelOrigin field preserves original creator attribution (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI)
as informational metadata separate from the serving provider (GitHub Models).

commit.message:
fix: startup unmute watcher killing daemon after 60s timeout

Two bugs in _startup_unmute_watcher introduced in smooth restart:
1. unmute_task was in asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED) — its completion
   after 60s timeout killed the daemon. Now fire-and-forget.
2. Unconditional unmute on timeout even when no audio streamed —
   now skips unmute if audio_streaming was never True.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: audit unwrap() calls and add ErrorMessage component

Task 1 — Rust unwrap() audit:
- watcher.rs: Replace 8 Mutex::lock().unwrap() calls with lock_store()
  helper that recovers from mutex poisoning via into_inner()
- helm.rs: Replace as_object().unwrap() with is_some_and() pattern;
  add // safe: comment on literal regex unwrap
- main.rs: Add // safe: comment on parent().unwrap() (always has parent)

Task 2 — Error state UX:
- Create ErrorMessage.svelte with icon, suggestion, and retry button
- Create error-suggestions.ts mapping common K8s errors to user-friendly
  suggestions (connection refused, 401/403, timeout, kubelogin, etc.)
- Wire ErrorMessage into pods, helm, and overview pages replacing raw
  error text
- Add unit tests for getSuggestion helper (8 tests)

commit.message:
fix: harden port-forward limits, exec output cap, and schema migrations

Port forward (S7 — #103):
- Add AtomicUsize counter, reject if >10 concurrent forwards
- Validate remote_port in range 1-65535
- Wrap accept loop with 1-hour idle timeout
- Decrement counter when forward task ends

Exec output (S8 — #104):
- Replace read_to_end with bounded_read (max 10MB per stream)
- Wrap entire exec in 5-minute timeout
- Return clear error messages for both limits

Schema migration (#105):
- Add schema_version table in init()
- Add get/set_schema_version helpers
- Add migrate() runner called on open() after init()
- Version 1 = current schema; future migrations slot in sequentially

Closes #103, closes #104, closes #105

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update git submodules

* Update manila from branch 'master'
  to e6a04c9b64f976520791dddb628f5e968b83c8a8
  - Merge "Fix generic driver NFS share deletion 'target is busy' error"
  - Fix generic driver NFS share deletion 'target is busy' error
    
    When deleting NFS shares from generic driver service VMs, the
    operation can fail with 'target is busy' because NFS exports are
    not unexported before unmount is attempted.
    
    This patch fixes the issue by:
    
    1. Implementing NFSHelper.remove_exports() to actively unexport all
       NFS clients via 'exportfs -u' before the share is unmounted.
       Previously this method was a no-op.
    
    2. Adding a lazy unmount fallback in GenericShareDriver._unmount_device()
       that detects 'is busy' errors and retries with 'umount -l' to allow
       the unmount to succeed even when NFS kernel state hasn't fully
       drained.
    
    Assisted-By: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
    Change-Id: I9e31a0e082c50ab24b3f01522aba7bb1fb2eaa64
    Signed-off-by: Yaguang Tang <heut2008@gmail.com>

refactor: remove legacy move-sink-input routing from bridge_daemon

PULSE_SINK env var in each subprocess handles routing from first sample.
The _ensure_sink_routing() call on stream start was a pre-PULSE_SINK
leftover that could itself cause PA glitches triggering re-anchoring.

Removed: _ensure_sink_routing(), _sink_routed flag, amove_pid_sink_inputs
import, os import. amove_pid_sink_inputs remains in pulse.py for diagnostics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: smooth restart — mute sinks, cache sink names, auto-unmute

Three improvements for glitch-free service restarts:

1. Startup sink mute: daemon_process mutes PA sink before BridgeDaemon
   starts, _startup_unmute_watcher unmutes after audio_streaming=True
   + 1.5s stabilization delay (60s timeout safety)

2. Pre-restart local mute: saveAndRestart() mutes all local PA sinks
   via force_local before stopping — no MA pause/play needed, so sync
   group members on other bridges keep playing uninterrupted

3. Sink name cache: configure_bluetooth_audio() saves found sink name
   to LAST_SINKS[mac] in config.json; on restart tries cached name
   first, skipping 3s A2DP delay + retries if still valid

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Services: EventKitService (@Observable, full CRUD, iOS 17 access), CategoryService

- Views: ContentView (TabView), MonthView, DayView (timeline), WeekStripView
- Views: EventCard, EventDetailView, EventEditView
- Components: ADHDColorPalette, TimeBlockView, CategoryBadge
- Utilities: DateExtensions, ColorExtensions (ADHD muted palette)
- Tests: ADHDCalendarTests (35+ unit tests), ADHDCalendarUITests
